source_name: NICE CG117 Appendix L (2011) inflated to 2023 GBP via NHS cost inflation index
notes: NICE CG117 Appendix L (2011): outpatient care (active TB) £777 + contact tracing £2,058 = £2,835/episode in 2008/09 GBP (NHS financial year). Inflated to 2023 GBP via NHS cost inflation (HCHS PSSRU 2009-2016 x CPIH 2016-2023 combined factor ~1.55); applied factor ×1.63 gives £4,622/episode ÷ 6 months = £770/month (GBP, pounds sterling). Note: ×1.63 slightly exceeds CPIH-only estimate (×1.51); may reflect NHS-specific labour/capital cost inflation running above general CPI in 2009-2016 (HCHS 2009->2016 = ×1.22 vs CPIH ×1.09 for same period). Conservative: White & Jit 2015 (NICE NG33 Appendix I2, Table 2.5) cross-check gives £5,329 in 2014 GBP (~£7,100 in 2023 GBP, £1,183/month) — our estimate excludes inpatient/DOT components.

notes: TWO-PHASE REACTIVATION: Berrocal-Almanza 2022 rate (0.000912/mo = 1.09%/yr) applies years 0-5 (cycles 1-60); this lower rate applies years 5-55 (cycles 61-660). Rationale: Berrocal-Almanza 2022 measured short-term follow-up in newly-arrived IGRA+ migrants — extrapolating to 55yr gives 45% lifetime reactivation vs literature ~10-15%. Horsburgh 2004 NEJM: reactivation declines after first 2yr; remote LTBI rate ~0.10-0.40%/yr. Dale 2022 uses same two-phase approach (Horsburgh 2004). 0.000333/mo = 0.40%/yr gives ~22% lifetime reactivation; 0.000167/mo = 0.20%/yr gives ~14%. TO DISABLE two-phase behaviour: set equal to p_LatentUndiagnosed_ActiveUndiagnosed (0.000912) — scale factor becomes 1.0 and no change occurs at cycle 61.

parameter_name: IGRA testing programme delivery cost - cough screen
source_name: Snoad et al. 2025 medRxiv (preprint); NICE NG33 Appendix K; model estimate
source_type: Preprint (UK micro-costing); Clinical guideline; Model estimate
notes: ⚠ MODEL ESTIMATE: one-time IGRA testing programme delivery cost per 100,000 migrants for parallel IGRA strategy with cough-only symptom screen. Covers IGRA reagents, phlebotomy, staff time, admin, clinic costs. Base = £300,000/100k (£3/pp). EMPIRICAL BENCHMARK (Snoad et al. 2025 medRxiv v2 — PREPRINT, not peer-reviewed; 5 secondary care TB clinics, England): full secondary care LTBI encounter cost £64.11/person (IGRA-negative) to £142.86/person (IGRA-positive); phlebotomy ~£8.50; indirect costs = 70% of direct staff costs. Implied programme cost for cough trigger (~10–15% of 100k migrants = 10,000–15,000 tested at ~£30–40/person streamlined community cost) ≈ £300k–£600k — consistent with base case. Note: Snoad figures are secondary care costs; community phlebotomy + IGRA only would be lower. No UKHSA/NHS England programme-level cost data published. gamma(shape=16, scale=18750): mean=300000 sd=75000; 95% range ≈ £175k–£445k. DSA range: £50k–£6M (£0.50–£60/pp); breakeven ≈ £130M/100k (£1,300/pp; far off-chart). LOW CONFIDENCE.

parameter_name: IGRA testing programme delivery cost - all-symptom screen
source_name: Snoad et al. 2025 medRxiv (preprint); NICE NG33 Appendix K; model estimate
source_type: Preprint (UK micro-costing); Clinical guideline; Model estimate
notes: ⚠ MODEL ESTIMATE: one-time IGRA testing programme delivery cost per 100,000 migrants for parallel IGRA strategy with all-symptom screen (broader eligible population than cough-only). Covers IGRA reagents, phlebotomy, staff time, admin, clinic costs. Base = £500,000/100k (£5/pp). EMPIRICAL BENCHMARK (Snoad et al. 2025 medRxiv v2 — PREPRINT): £64.11/person (IGRA-negative) to £142.86/person (IGRA-positive); cost per LTBI diagnosis £458.32. Implied programme cost for all-symptom trigger (~20–25% of 100k migrants = 20,000–25,000 tested at ~£30–40/person) ≈ £600k–£1M — base case (£500k) is at lower end; may be an underestimate. No UKHSA/NHS England programme-level cost data publicly available. gamma(shape=16, scale=31250): mean=500000 sd=125000; 95% range ≈ £290k–£740k. DSA range: £50k–£6M; breakeven ≈ £130M/100k (£1,300/pp). LOW CONFIDENCE.

parameter_name: IGRA LTBI sensitivity - QFT-TB Gold Plus (pooled)
source_name: Zenner et al. 2025 Eur Respir J Table 1
notes: Pooled LTBI sensitivity for QFT-TB Gold Plus from umbrella review of 437 original studies across 32 systematic reviews (Zenner et al. 2025 Eur Respir J). Used to compute LTBI detection for parallel IGRA strategies: n_ltbi × 0.83 = 14,774/100k. beta(72.4, 14.8): mean=0.83, sd=0.04; 95% range ≈ 0.75–0.91.

source_name: Zenner et al. 2025 Eur Respir J Table 1
notes: Pooled LTBI sensitivity for T-SPOT.TB from umbrella review of 437 original studies across 32 systematic reviews (Zenner et al. 2025 Eur Respir J). Used to compute LTBI detection for parallel IGRA strategies: n_ltbi × 0.88 = 15,664/100k. T-SPOT.TB shows slightly higher pooled sensitivity than QFT-TB. beta(57.2, 7.8): mean=0.88, sd=0.04; 95% range ≈ 0.80–0.95.

parameter_name: IGRA specificity - QFT-TB Gold Plus (BCG-vaccinated populations)
source_name: Pai et al. 2008 Ann Intern Med
notes: Pooled QFT-GIT specificity in BCG-vaccinated populations from Pai et al. 2008 meta-analysis (Ann Intern Med 149:177-184): 96% (95% CI 94-98%). Lower than non-BCG populations (~99%). Used to correct LTBI detection formula for parallel IGRA strategies: LatentDiagnosed = n_ltbi × sens - (1 - spec) × n_uninfected. At 0.96: FP = 0.04 × 81,200 = 3,248/100k. beta(91.2, 3.8): mean=0.96, sd=0.02; 95% range ≈ 0.92-0.99. SA range: 0.90-1.00.

source_name: Pai et al. 2008 Ann Intern Med
notes: Pooled T-SPOT.TB specificity in BCG-vaccinated populations from Pai et al. 2008 meta-analysis (Ann Intern Med 149:177-184): 93% (95% CI 86-100%). Lower than QFT in BCG-vaccinated populations due to slightly higher cross-reactivity. Used to correct LTBI detection: LatentDiagnosed = n_ltbi × sens - (1 - spec) × n_uninfected. At 0.93: FP = 0.07 × 81,200 = 5,684/100k; net LTBI det. = 9,980/100k (vs 15,664 at spec=1.00). Wider CI reflects smaller BCG-vaccinated dataset for T-SPOT. beta(150.43, 11.32): mean=0.93, sd=0.02; 95% range ≈ 0.89-0.97. SA range: 0.90-1.00.
